ddls:
CREATE TABLE "products" (
  "product_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each product */,
  "product_name" TEXT /* Name of the product */,
  "description" TEXT /* Detailed description of the product */,
  "category" TEXT /* Category of the product (e.g., luggage, backpacks) */,
  "brand" TEXT /* Brand of the product */,
  "sku" TEXT /* Stock Keeping Unit (SKU) of the product */,
  "weight" REAL /* Weight of the product in pounds */,
  "dimensions" TEXT /* Dimensions of the product in inches (L x W x H) */,
  "material" TEXT /* Material of the product */,
  "color" TEXT /* Color of the product */,
  "image_url" TEXT /* URL of the product image */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Date and time the product was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Date and time the product was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("product_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., customer, administrator, moderator) */,
  "password_hash" TEXT /* Hashed password for the user */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Date and time the user was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Date and time the user was last updated */,
  "last_login_at" TEXT /* Date and time the user last logged in */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "amazon_products" (
  "amazon_product_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each Amazon product */,
  "product_id" INTEGER /* ID of the product in the platform's database */,
  "amazon_product_name" TEXT /* Name of the product on Amazon */,
  "price" REAL /* Price of the product on Amazon */,
  "deal_url" TEXT /* URL of the deal on Amazon */,
  "prime_shipping" INTEGER /* Whether the product is eligible for Prime shipping */,
  "free_returns" INTEGER /* Whether the product offers free returns */,
  "amazon_rating" REAL /* Amazon rating of the product */,
  "amazon_reviews" INTEGER /* Number of Amazon reviews for the product */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("amazon_product_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_amazon_products_product_id FOREIGN KEY ("product_id") REFERENCES products ("product_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "amazon_api_keys" (
  "api_key_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each API key */,
  "api_key" TEXT /* Amazon API key */,
  "api_secret_key" TEXT /* Amazon API secret key */,
  "access_token" TEXT /* Access token for authentication */,
  "refresh_token" TEXT /* Refresh token for authentication */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Date and time the API key was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Date and time the API key was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("api_key_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "ratings" (
  "rating_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each rating */,
  "product_id" INTEGER /* ID of the product being rated */,
  "average_rating" REAL /* Average rating of the product */,
  "total_reviews" INTEGER /* Total number of reviews for the product */,
  "total_ratings" INTEGER /* Total number of ratings for the product */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Date and time the rating was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Date and time the rating was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("rating_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_ratings_product_id FOREIGN KEY ("product_id") REFERENCES products ("product_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "search_history" (
  "search_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each search */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user who performed the search */,
  "search_query" TEXT /* Search query entered by the user */,
  "search_results" TEXT /* Comma-separated list of search result IDs */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Date and time the search was performed */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("search_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_search_history_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "product_categories" (
  "category_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each category */,
  "product_id" INTEGER /* ID of the product being categorized */,
  "category_name" TEXT /* Name of the category */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("category_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_product_categories_product_id FOREIGN KEY ("product_id") REFERENCES products ("product_id")
)
